faq.a3: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your files never leave your device. You can verify this by opening DevTools (F12) and checking the Network tab — zero upload requests.

faq.a1: URL encoding converts special characters into percent-encoded format (e.g., space becomes %20) so they can be safely used in web addresses.

faq.q1: How is JSON mapped to XML?
faq.a1: Object keys become XML element names. Arrays produce repeated elements with the same name. Primitive values become text content.
faq.a2: XML attributes are not preserved in the JSON output. Only element names and text content are converted.
